channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: For some, the pope's big Holy Year only aggravates housing crisis as overtourism pushes out Romans
item.description: When Pope Francis formally opens the Holy Year next week, he will launch a dizzying 12-month calendar of events that include special Jubilee Masses for the faithful from all walks of life.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield And Isaia Montelione, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden to travel to Italy for meetings with pope, Meloni in January
item.description: President Biden will travel to Italy in January for meetings with Pope Francis, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Italian President Sergio Mattarella. The trip, from Jan. 9 to 12, will likely be his last foreign trip as president before President-elect Trump is sworn in on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: France's Macron says tour of cyclone-devastated Mayotte is a day he won't forget
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron arrived Thursday in the Indian Ocean island of Mayotte to survey Cyclone Chido’s destruction and was immediately confronted with a first-hand account of devastation across the French territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Putin boasts of gains in Ukraine at annual news conference meant to reinforce his authority
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — President Vladimir Putin boasted that his military operation in Ukraine has strengthened Russia and denied that the ouster of key ally Bashar Assad in Syria had hurt Moscow’s prestige, as he took questions at his annual news conference and call-in show Thursday.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau to shuffle his Cabinet Friday amid resignation calls and rising discontent
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Embattled Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau will shuffle his Cabinet Friday, two senior officials familiar with the matter said Thursday. The officials confirmed the shuffle and spoke on condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak publicly on the matter.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syrian villagers near the Golan Heights say Israeli forces are banning them from their fields
item.description: Israeli forces have set up a position in an abandoned Syrian army base in the village of Maariyah and prevented local farmers from accessing their fields, residents said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and HUSSEIN MALLA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Putin's annual press conference
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin talked for more than three hours during an annual news conference in Moscow on Thursday, discussing key topics including the war in Ukraine, his interest in dialogue with President-elect Trump and an openness to asking about detained journalist Austin Tice in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: France rape trial: Gisèle Pelicot speaks out after ex-husband, 50 others found guilty
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot spoke of her “very difficult ordeal” after 51 men were all found guilty Thursday in the drugging-and-rape trial that turned her into a feminist hero, expressing support for other victims of sexual violence whose cases don't get such attention and “whose stories remain untold.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Number of dead and missing still unclear as first aid arrives in quake-hit Pacific nation of Vanuatu
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Amid the havoc wrought by a violent earthquake two days earlier, Ivan Oswald and his staff prepared for lunchtime service Thursday at Nambawan Cafe, on an idyllic stretch of Vanuatu’s waterfront.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Russia threats loom, Finland's people are learning to shoot back
item.description: KERAVA, Finland (AP) — Unsettled by Russia's expansionism and emboldened by its recent accession to NATO, Finland is rallying to strengthen its national self-defense beyond its traditional military capabilities. The popularity of weapons training in the Nordic country has soared in recent months.
item.dc:creator: JAMES BROOKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinian Authority under pressure amid rising resistance, popularity of Iran-backed terror groups
item.description: Palestinian Authority’s crackdown on terrorists intensifies, but efforts to regain control of the city and surrounding areas face fierce resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ireland embraced data centers that the AI boom needs. Now they're consuming too much of its energy
item.description: Dozens of massive data centers are consuming more electricity than all of the urban homes in Ireland and starting to wear out the warm welcome that brought them here.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken has announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during his likely final trip to the United Nations while in office.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China skirts US efforts to stiff-arm CCP interference by bolstering state, local relations
item.description: China is skirting U.S. efforts to block its interference in areas ranging from trade to national security by cozying up with state officials to foster relations and keep its influence active in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot thanks backers after her ex-husband and his co-defendants are convicted in rape trial
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot said after 51 men were convicted in the drugging-and-rape trial that riveted France and turned her into an icon that the ordeal was “very difficult” and expressed support for other victims of sexual violence.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Tom Nouvian And Marine Lesprit, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Survivors recount 'very apocalyptic' cyclone that ripped through French territory of Mayotte
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — They clustered around water taps, rare sources of electricity and each other.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Researcher: North Korea can produce ballistic missiles for Russia to use against Ukraine in months
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — North Korea demonstrated this year that it could produce ballistic missiles and supply them to Russia for use against Ukraine in a matter of months, the head of a research organization that traces weapons used in the war said Wednesday. Jonah Leff told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British PM Starmer urges Trump to stand with Ukraine
item.description: The United Kingdom’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer is urging President-elect Trump to stand with Ukraine in its war against Russia. Starmer and Trump spoke on the phone Wednesday, their second call since Trump won the presidential election last month.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: 51 percent oppose Trump tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China: Poll
item.description: Slightly more than half, 51 percent, of voters in the U.S. oppose President-elect Trump's plan to impose tariffs on imported goods from Mexico, Canada and China, a Quinnipiac University poll revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders another Gaza evacuation ahead of an offensive
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered Palestinians to evacuate another section of central Gaza ahead of an offensive in the built-up Bureij refugee camp, even as Israel and the Hamas appeared to inch closer to a ceasefire in the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's Syria exit could help Ukraine-Israel relationship as analyst warns it 'offers little' to Jerusalem
item.description: With Russia’s diminished influence in Syria, Ukraine calls on Israel to reconsider its stance and provide essential defensive weapons and technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia detains suspect accused of killing high-ranking general in Moscow
item.description: A suspect has been taken into custody in connection with the Tuesday killing of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov and his assistant in Moscow, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will occupy border territory in Syria for now
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces will for now occupy territory they had seized over the border in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian official takes credit after Russian general Igor Kirillov killed by explosive device in Moscow
item.description: An official with the Ukrainian Security Services says the agency was behind the bombing assassination of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov, commander of Russia’s chemical, biological and radiation defense forces, and his assistant in Moscow on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian Finance Minister resigns as Trudeau government's popularity flounders
item.description: Canadian Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland announced her resignation from Justin Trudeau's Cabinet on Monday, a powerful blow to the Liberal Prime Minister's hold on the government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz loses no-confidence vote, election called for early next year
item.description: In November, Scholz's government collapsed after the Free Democrat Party quit over the country's debt. An early election to replace Scholz will occur on Feb. 23 following Monday's no-confidence vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to say ‘Merry Christmas’ in 10 languages to friends around the world
item.description: This guide will pique your curiosity with how to say "Merry Christmas" in different languages; plus, how Christmas is celebrated in various countries around the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assad paints himself as 'custodian' to Syria as picture unfolds on collapse of Damascus
item.description: Former Syrian President Bashar al-Assad broke his silence since fleeing the nation following the Islamist rebel takeover and looked to portray himself only ever as a "custodian" of Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN talks fail to reach agreement on dealing with rising risk of global drought
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Despite two weeks of U.N.-sponsored talks in Saudi Arabia’s Riyadh, the participating 197 nations failed to agree early Saturday on a plan to deal with global droughts, made longer and more severe by a warming climate.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation
